title: carex vulgaris var. tricostata
intro: Carex vulgaris var. tricostata, also known as the lesser hairy sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The lesser hairy sedge has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Carex vulgaris var. tricostata include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to promote respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
